# flashduty_silence_rule (Resource)

Manages a Flashduty silence rule for a channel.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Create a silence rule with a fixed time window
resource "flashduty_silence_rule" "maintenance" {
  channel_id  = 6148622168454
  rule_name   = "Weekly Maintenance Window"
  description = "Silence alerts during weekly maintenance"

  filters = [
    {
      conditions = [
        {
          key  = "severity"
          oper = "IN"
          vals = ["Warning", "Info"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]

  time_filter = {
    start_time = 1773121380
    end_time   = 1773207780
  }
}

# Create a silence rule with recurring time filters
resource "flashduty_silence_rule" "known_issue" {
  channel_id  = 6148622168454
  rule_name   = "Known Disk Space Alert"
  description = "Silence known disk space alerts from legacy server"

  filters = [
    {
      conditions = [
        {
          key  = "labels.host"
          oper = "IN"
          vals = ["legacy-server-01"]
        },
        {
          key  = "title"
          oper = "IN"
          vals = ["disk space"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]

  time_filters = [
    {
      start  = "02:00"
      end    = "04:00"
      repeat = [0]
    }
  ]

  is_directly_discard = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `channel_id` (Number) The ID of the channel this rule belongs to.
- `description` (String) The description of the rule.
- `filters` (Attributes List) The filter conditions (OR groups of AND conditions). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--filters))
- `rule_name` (String) The name of the silence rule.

### Optional

- `is_directly_discard` (Boolean) Whether to directly discard matching alerts.
- `priority` (Number) The priority of the silence rule for ordering.
- `time_filter` (Attributes) Single time range (mutually exclusive with `time_filters`). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--time_filter))
- `time_filters` (Attributes List) Recurring time filters (mutually exclusive with `time_filter`). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--time_filters))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the silence rule.

<a id="nestedatt--filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filters`

Required:

- `conditions` (Attributes List) AND conditions within this group. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--filters--conditions))

<a id="nestedatt--filters--conditions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filters.conditions`

Required:

- `key` (String) The attribute or label key (e.g., `title`, `severity`, `labels.xxx`).
- `oper` (String) The operator: `IN` or `NOTIN`.
- `vals` (List of String) The values to match.



<a id="nestedatt--time_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `time_filter`

Required:

- `end_time` (Number) End timestamp.
- `start_time` (Number) Start timestamp.


<a id="nestedatt--time_filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `time_filters`

Required:

- `end` (String) End time (e.g., `23:59`).
- `repeat` (List of Number) Days of week (0=Sunday, 1-6=Monday-Saturday).
- `start` (String) Start time (e.g., `10:00`).
